problem text: What is the expected number of times a fair $12$-sided die, numbered $1-12$, must be rolled until an odd number is rolled?
problem solution: With $12$ possible outcomes, $6$ are odd, giving a $\dfrac{1}{2}$ chance of rolling an odd number on each roll. Therefore, the number of rolls needed to achieve an odd result follows $M \sim \text{Geom}(1/2)$, resulting in an expected value of $2$.
problem hint: Half of the die's values are odd numbers
